displayTitle: Mapping the Beam: Near Field to Far Field

beats.narration: Forget the tidy flashlight cone. Your ultrasonic beam splits into a turbulent near field and a predictable far field. Position decides echo height.

payload.title: THE SOUND BEAM
payload.sub: Where echo amplitude tells the truth — and where it lies.

beats.narration: The element vibrates as thousands of sources, and their wavelets interfere. Inside the near field, amplitude swings wildly. Beyond N it decays predictably.

payload.heading: Two zones, one boundary: N
payload.note: AMPLITUDE SWINGS INSIDE N · SETTLES BEYOND

beats.narration: N is D squared over four wavelengths. The half angle's sine: one point two two lambda over D. Raise diameter or frequency: longer, narrower beam.
In practice: evaluate beyond the near field, peak the echo before judging, and overlap each pass; the beam edge reads half.
Here's the rule: amplitude is only trustworthy beyond N, on axis. Calculate your beam before the job, and the echoes make sense.

payload.sub: Calculate N and the spread before the probe touches the part.
